posts.content: maybe you grind because your body is trying to clear the deuterium in your sleep because you aren't doing it yourself, its a last ditch attempt to start the clearing process. Maybe the mastic will act as a buffer (cartiledge) between the edges of your teeth and so doing it in the day, will reduce the body's need to do it your sleep. On the other side have a look at deep seated trauma and anger suppression that also might be cause you grit your teeth and hold it all in.

posts.thread_title: I Stay Away...From nnEMF
posts.author: Mr.AJ
posts.content: Walk around the outside and see where it is coming from. Normally one side is the strongest, then look for towers. If no towers on that side close by good chance it is the neighbors WiFi. In single story buildings shielding the fence with wire mesh (metal window screen) can be a quick easy fix.

posts.thread_title: ATLANTIC THERMOHALINE AMOC
posts.author: Jack Kruse
posts.content: New satellite data has revealed a surprising reversal deep inside Earth. In 2010, a large region of molten iron in the planet’s outer core beneath the equatorial Pacific Ocean abruptly changed direction, shifting from a slow westward flow to a strong eastward movement. This discovery, led by researchers at the University of Edinburgh, shows that the liquid outer core is far more dynamic and unpredictable than previously thought. The flow appears to have weakened again since around 2020, but the event challenges long-held assumptions about the stability of Earth’s deep interior. The outer core, located roughly 2,900 kilometers (1,800 miles) beneath our feet, consists of superheated liquid iron and nickel. Its motion drives Earth’s magnetic field through the geodynamo process, the same field that protects us from harmful solar radiation and enables navigation and satellite operations. Using high-precision magnetic measurements from ESA’s Swarm mission, along with data from CryoSat, CHAMP, Ørsted, and ground observatories, scientists were able to reconstruct these deep flows with unprecedented accuracy. While the exact cause of the reversal remains unknown, it provides valuable new insights into the complex interactions between the outer core, inner core, and mantle. CITES Madsen, F. D., et al. (2026). Principal component analysis of the 2010 reversal of core-surface flow beneath the Pacific Ocean. Journal of Studies of Earth’s Deep Interior

posts.thread_title: Surfer’s Ear: ear health tips?  - safest way for me to clean my ears?
posts.author: riksa
posts.content: There has to be a lot of marketing bs out there as far as devices go. I think there is overkill and overpricing, even Jack says you can just get a TENS unit. A TENS unit with some settings and earclip electrodes 70€, or used and DIY like me for 30€ total. Or you can buy nurosym for 700€ which is basically the same thing but with an integrated app and marketing for tVNS. If money is no issue there is no reason to overthink it, just buy the tVNS device you like, they'll likely all be customizable adequately.

posts.thread_title: Albert's Optimal Journal with wife and toddlers
posts.author: AlbertW
posts.content: Toddler eczema still isn't improving much. Recently I read Jack's blog about building solar callus. - IR-A, morning sun: we've been getting toddler into the sun around sunrise - feeding him a lot of king salmon collar recently: he likes it and it's super high in DHA - going to try out seafood exoskeleton broth We just started supplementing with L-histidine yesterday, hoping to help with the filaggrin. There are multiple studies showing positive impact and no side effects. Of course getting it via AM sun exposure is best, but we're hoping that this will help him along.
